summary: Backend (11/11) + Frontend smoke testing PASS for the new Comparative Reports PDF/Excel export. Endpoints stream valid PDF (%PDF-1.x, parseable, ~19KB) and multi-sheet XLSX (Summary, By Asset Type, Peer Matrix, Drilldown, Defective Only, Last Inspections, Remarks). Section toggles, card_c_full, drill_state, dept-filter, and SUP-role peer anonymisation all honored. Frontend dialog renders all 7 toggles with correct defaults (card_c_full unchecked, others checked), style buttons work, network call captured to correct endpoint with correct JSON payload.
critical_code_review_comments: comparative_export.py is 1005 lines — slightly over the 700-line guideline. Could be split into modules (e.g., _pdf.py, _excel.py, _data_bundle.py) for maintainability, but logic is well-organised and section-commented so not blocking.

ExportRequest model uses string union ('window_days', 'stat', 'style') without Literal/Enum validation — invalid values will silently propagate to data layer. Consider Literal['7','15','30','90','fy','all'] for window_days and Literal['median','mean'] for stat.
_section_a_rows references PALETTE imported from comparative.py but never uses it; PALETTE is consumed only via type_palette dict → unused dependency in Card A.

_last_inspection_rows_sync (lines 298-300) is dead code — function returns None as placeholder and is never called. _section_last_inspection_rows would crash if invoked because it returns None. Dead path; safe to remove or replaced by the working async _last_inspection_rows.

_resolved_repair_hours is called with a single asset id string in _build_excel:899 (card_c_full path). Verify it accepts a str (typical signatures take a set of ids). If it expects a collection, single-asset rows will silently produce empty stats. (Tests pass because card_c_full sheet is generated; just flagging for E1 to confirm by spot-checking values).

PDF radar drawing is embedded as a Drawing flowable but no caption/legend is printed near it — peer rows show in matrix table only. Acceptable but boardroom-style PDF could benefit from a legend explaining colors (self=teal, peers=blue) directly under the chart.
Excel — when card_c_current is False but card_c_full is True the 'Drilldown' sheet is omitted (only 'Drilldown — Full' is created). Documented behavior; consistent with the elif in _build_excel.

Both _build_pdf and _build_excel call _build_data_bundle independently — quick-download flow that hits PDF then Excel re-fetches everything. Not a correctness bug, just a perf note for users who request both.

ComparativeExportDialog: download() does NOT pass Authorization header; relies on cookie-less axios + unauthenticated POST. Backend endpoints currently accept the request without an auth header (enforced only via user_id path param). Confirm this is intentional — per RBAC policy other endpoints require Bearer token.
ComparativeExportDialog uses checkbox 'data-state' attribute reflection (Radix), so toggle states are testable via [data-testid]; verified via Playwright.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (dialog opens, all 7 toggles + 2 style buttons render with correct defaults, download button captured POST to correct endpoint with sections payload)

test_credentials: SA001/admin123 (verified). SSE001/admin123 (verified — Peer Matrix anonymises peers as 'Peer N', self row keeps real name 'Ramprakash Barla' with ★ marker).
seed_data_creation: None — read-only export tests using existing seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend export endpoints: POST /api/reports/comparative/export/{pdf|excel}/{user_id}. ExportRequest body has window_days, stat, dept_id, asset_type_ids, drill_state{level,parent_id,parent_asset_type_id}, sections{card_a,card_b,card_c_current,card_c_full,defective,remarks,last_inspection}, style. Default sections: card_c_full=False, others=True. Excel sheets when defaults: Summary, By Asset Type, Peer Matrix, Drilldown, Defective Only, Last Inspections, Remarks. With card_c_full=True (and card_c_current can stay True or False), an extra/replacement sheet 'Drilldown — Full' is added (if both True, 'Drilldown — Full' wins per current code). Comparative drill GET endpoint is /api/reports/comparative/grouped/{user_id} (NOT grouped-drilldown). For SUP anonymisation visual check, login as SSE001 — Peer Matrix col A shows 'Peer 1', 'Peer 2', etc., self row shows real name + ★. Run: python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_comparative_export.py -v
